sections.paragraph:
τρυφερός, ά, όν (Eur., Thu. et al.; BGU 1080, 18; LXX; TestGad 1:4 of Joseph, who was ‘frail’, i.e. not tough like his brothers; Philo, Somn. 2, 9; SibOr 3, 527) pert. to being gentle and free from harshness, delicate, gentle, subdued, of the Holy Spirit Hm 5, 1, 3; 5, 2, 6 (in these pass. in contrast to a spirit of bitterness). Of the ἄγγελος τῆς δικαιοσύνης 6, 2, 3. Of unmarried women Hs 9, 2, 5 (Chariton 2, 2, 2; cp. Sus 31, but there in ref. to a woman’s voluptuousness).—DELG s.v. θρύπτω III.

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestGad TestGad = Testament of Gad, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Hm Hm = Mandates

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Chariton Chariton , scriptor eroticus (writer of romances) I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

ref. ref. = reference(s)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1018
